obligations.obligation:
e) Direct the respondents to compensate the indirect loss incurred by the petitioner on
account of purchase of power from other sources to meet the “quantum of power”
reduced by respondents on account of ISTS losses due to non-compliance of
Regulation 13(11) of Sharing Regulation 2020.

These Regulations are required to be
complied with by all statutory bodies and Designated ISTS Customers (DICs).

As per the NLDC procedure, transmission charges and losses in
respect of ISTS lines are required to be calculated on the basis of the LTA / MTOA
quantum relating to the ISTS network used by a DIC for the drawal of power from the
CGSs using ISTS lines.

This quantum of LTA /MTOA is required to be furnished by
CTUIL to NLDC.

h) As per the Procedure for Collecting Data and Information for determining Inter-
State Transmission Charges and Losses, CTUIL is required to furnish the break-up
details of LTA/MTOA for all the generating stations connected to both the ISTS and
intra-State transmission systems.

r) The drawal details at every node from Interstate Generating Stations using
ISTS lines and Intra-STS concerning the applicant are required to be brought on record
by NLDC for the proper adjudication of the issues raised by the applicant in the
accompanying Petition seeking to exclude the entire qu...

Commission vide previous RoPs, the CTUIL did not furnish the data pertaining to a
detailed breakup of the LTA/MTOA in respect of all the generating stations connected

(5) Transmission charges under AC-BC shall be shared by all drawee DICs and injecting

(3) MTC covered under AC-UBC shall be apportioned on transmission lines of the Basic

Such
apportionment shall be made on per circuit kilometer basis for each conductor configuration
at each voltage level as per the methodology specified in Annexure-I of these regulations,
for obtaining transmission line-wise transmission charges for each conductor configuration

Regulation 13(11) of the Sharing Regulations, 2020 provides as under:
“(11) Where a generating station is connected to both ISTS and intra-State transmission
system, only ISTS charges and losses shall be applicable on the quantum of Long-
Term Access and Medium-Term Open Access corresponding to capa...

We observe from the recommendation of the Sub-Group formed under SRPC
Secretariat and the deliberation at SRPC, the methodology adopted by SRPC for
determining the quantum of LTA and MTOA corresponding to the capacity connected to
the ISTS for the dual connected generators, mainly lies on the princi...

The PPA clearly provided that KPTCL shall construct the transmission lines
from Udupi (then Nagarjunasagar) to 220 KV Kemar/Kuvoor and 400 kV Hassan.

However,
the moot question which arises for our consideration is how much capacity of Udupi is to
be considered as connected to ISTS under Regulation 13(11) of the 2020 Sharing
Regulations?

In this regard, we have perused the principles of the adequacy of the STU system
recommended by the sub-group of LTA for consideration under Regulation 13(11) of the
2020 Sharing Regulations quoted as follows:
“i) The intra-STU network planned and implemented at the time of commissioning of the
conc...

We direct that NLDC shall calculate the quantum of LTA to be exempted for payment
of ISTS charges towards the LTA quantum of Udupi from the date of effectiveness of the
2020 Sharing Regulations, based on the above quoted principles.

Accordingly, the RTA/ RTDA shall be revised by SRPC within a period of 30 days
thereafter.”
In the above order, to calculate the capacity connected with the ISTS, the principles
suggested by the sub-group of SRPC have been relied upon.

It is noted that the
methodology to determine the capacity connected to the ISTS for Regulation 13(11) of
Sharing Regulations,2020, nowhere relies on actual drawal data by the State.

The issue involved in the Petition pertains to the period from 1.11.2020 to 31.10.2023
to determine the capacity connected to the ISTS for the purpose of Regulation 13(11) of the
Sharing Regulations, 2020.

Regulation 13(11) of
the Sharing Regulations provides that ISTS charges and losses shall apply only to the
quantum of LTA and MTOA corresponding to the capacity connected to ISTS.

The
methodology for the determination of capacity connected to the ISTS for the purpose of
Regulation 13(11) of the Sharing Regulations, as recommended by SRPC and considered
by the Commission in Petition No. 224/MP/2022, has no relevance to the actual drawal by
the State from the corresponding gene...
